comment: Act-1 Swagbound story gate-chain (Signal Town = Onett). Battle gates are VISIBLE fixed overworld boss sprites: collide with the boss to play dialogue and start the battle; no battle starts from an invisible area trigger. Bosses are placed at their vanilla EarthBound districts: card clique = Frank (group 448) at the GAME arcade, downtown west of spawn; relay gate Returnless King = Cop+Runaway Dog (group 36, req clique_cleared, sets signal:route_open) on the road by the Police Station (in EB the cops guard the way up to Giant Step); first threshold Malady = Titanic Ant (group 450, req route_open, sets signal:threshold_cleared and source:first_witness) on the north road toward the Giant Step approach. NOTE: Cop+Runaway Dog is a roaming encounter in vanilla EB (no single canonical tile) and Titanic Ant is the far Giant Step summit boss, so those two are anchored to the matching Onett DISTRICT rather than an exact vanilla coordinate. The warning + solid barrier stay area/flag based as the police roadblock north of the Cop, opening on route_open. All three boss groups are unescapable (content/battle-rules.json); their setFlags + once-marker advance ONLY on a win. Dev: ?flags=signal:route_open to skip ahead.

dialogue: The arcade light flattens Bosch into a better-lit rumor. A clean copy of his face grins from a card nobody remembers printing.
MIFELLA 2: "You're late to your own read. The clique already traded the portable you."
These cards don't start here. They point up the line, toward the yard that prints them.
The gate files Bosch before it ever looks at him.
RETURNLESS KING: "Source. Vessel. Claimant. Derivative. Pick the lie that travels."
Bosch refuses the stamp. The route unlocks anyway -- not because it agreed, because he forced it.
The road north is open enough to see and closed enough to mean something.
A smiling line of route aides keeps checking the same card, never once looking at Bosch.
"The road opens once the gate decides which Bosch is leaving. Please hold."
The first threshold hums with a memory too small to sell.
MALADY: "A private thing is just a public thing with poor distribution. I'm only the symptom you can see."
The card warms, then fails to describe him. Bosch keeps the damaged version -- and the threshold records it.
